Other: 1850: Observation of distance to either bank by eye indicated this ship was very nearly on course line in center of channel but when bearings were taken on North Inian Light and Cape Spencer Light, and plotted, the lines of position did not cross in the channel. Upon chaning courses standing into Cross Sound, it was found that Cape Spencer Light bore 04 relative rather than the 13 relative it should have been A 9 westerly deviation error had apparently developed from cause unknown During the run down Cross Sound, attempts were made to check the compass with bearings, but no definite data could be obtained because the course had been changed ~ since last fix had been obtained off North Inian Light. Compass appeared to be erratic and totally unreliable
